changes_summary: Addressed FLAG-001 by adding a new Step 5 that rewrites `PaymentUICog._post_admin_approval_thread_update` to edit the shared status message instead of sending a new "queued for sending" note, and amends `_cleanup_admin_intent_messages` to filter `status_message_id` out of the deletion list so the shared message survives admin approval even when `receipt_prompt_message_id` points at it. Added two new tests for that path and renumbered the test step to Step 6. Addressed FLAG-002 by rewriting Step 2 so the column-absent retry lives inside a nested try inside the existing outer exception handler — `update_admin_payment_intent` still returns `None` on any non-`status_message_id` failure, preserving the fail-closed caller contract. Updated the test for Step 2 to explicitly verify the None-on-unrelated-errors contract. Expanded the Overview constraints section to call out both flag findings explicitly. No scope growth beyond what's strictly required to make the collapse safe.
questions: Where exactly should the first status message appear? The plan places it inside `_start_admin_payment_flow` (right when the test payment is queued), but it could instead appear at `_gate_existing_intent`/`_gate_fresh_intent_atomic` (earlier — at admin approval) or at the moment the admin confirms the DM. Which lifecycle point is the intended anchor?

Is there a preferred wording for the in-place status progression ('Verifying wallet…', 'Test confirmed, please reply confirmed once you see it', 'Confirmation received, awaiting admin approval', 'Payout queued for sending', 'Payout sending…', 'Payment sent.')? Defaults will be picked if not specified.

For the failure / manual-review branches, do you want the status message edited to a terminal failure line in-channel, or should failures stay silent in-channel and surface only in the admin DM? Current assumption is edit-in-place.

Is it acceptable to reuse `receipt_prompt_message_id` to point at the status message id (with Step 5 excluding it from cleanup), or would you rather the stale-sweep logic read `status_message_id` directly and leave `receipt_prompt_message_id` untouched?

success_criteria.criterion: `sql/admin_payment_intents.sql` gains an idempotent `add column if not exists status_message_id bigint;` block, matching the existing migration style.

`db_handler.update_admin_payment_intent` retries once with `status_message_id` removed when Supabase reports the column is unknown, **and** still returns `None` (not raises) on any unrelated failure so the existing fail-closed caller contract is preserved.
`PaymentWorkerCog._handle_terminal_payment` does not call `_notify_payment_result` when `producer == 'admin_chat'`, but still calls it for every other producer.

A full admin_chat payout (through both the admin_chat cog and the PaymentUICog admin-approval transition) produces exactly one channel `send` for the status message plus one final reply tagging the recipient — no separate 'queued test payment', 'confirmation received', 'queued for sending', or multi-line 'Payment sent.' card.

`PaymentUICog._post_admin_approval_thread_update` edits the existing status message (via `fetch_message` + `edit(..., suppress=True)`) whenever the intent has `status_message_id`, and only falls back to a fresh `channel.send(...)` when the status message cannot be fetched.

`PaymentUICog._cleanup_admin_intent_messages` excludes `intent['status_message_id']` from the ids it passes to `safe_delete_messages`, so the shared status message is never deleted even when `receipt_prompt_message_id` points at the same id.

The final 'Payment sent' message is a Discord reply to the status message, mentions the recipient with `<@id>`, and is sent with `suppress_embeds=True` so the Solana Explorer link does not render a link card.

`status_message_id` is persisted on `admin_payment_intents` the first time the status message is sent and is reused across every later edit within the same payout (test queued, test confirmed, receipt confirmed, admin-approved, payout sending, payout complete).
Admin DM notifications (`_notify_intent_admin` test/final branches) remain unchanged in both content and trigger points.

`tests/test_admin_payments.py` includes new coverage for: generic-card suppression (positive and negative producer), column-absent fallback in `update_admin_payment_intent` **plus** the preserved `None`-on-unrelated-error contract, single-status-message reuse across the full payout lifecycle, the final reply's recipient mention + suppress_embeds flag, `PaymentUICog._post_admin_approval_thread_update` editing rather than sending, and `_cleanup_admin_intent_messages` preserving the status message id.

No new fallback/defensive wrappers beyond the one narrow `status_message_id` retry and the one narrow `fetch_message` fallback in `_post_admin_approval_thread_update` — no generic 'ignore unknown column' helper.
The admin_chat status-message helper stays small (~40 lines) and is the single place that creates/edits the status message inside `AdminChatCog`.
Status wording is concise (single short line per transition), not multi-bullet blocks.
Manual end-to-end smoke on staging confirms one edited message + one tagged reply with no Solana embed card across the full admin approval path.

assumptions: The first status message is created inside `_start_admin_payment_flow` at the moment the test payment is queued. Earlier lifecycle points (admin approval DM, fresh-intent atomic gate) are out of scope unless the user picks another anchor.

`channel.send(..., suppress_embeds=True)` and `message.edit(..., suppress=True)` are supported by the current discord.py version in use — the codebase already relies on the same flag in `src/features/curating/curator.py:189-193`.
The status message lives in the same channel as the intent (`intent['channel_id']`). Cross-channel editing is not needed.
The final 'Payment sent' message is a Discord reply (`message.reply(...)`) to the status message, not a standalone `channel.send`.
Failure / manual-review branches edit the status message to a short terminal failure line rather than sending additional channel messages.

When the test is confirmed, we set `receipt_prompt_message_id = status_message_id` so the stale-receipt sweep (`_sweep_stale_test_receipts`) still has a marker. Step 5 excludes `status_message_id` from `_cleanup_admin_intent_messages` so this reuse is safe.

`PaymentUICog._post_admin_approval_thread_update` is allowed to fall back to its current `channel.send(...)` behavior when the status message cannot be fetched (NotFound, HTTPException, no id), so legacy rows and non-admin_chat edge cases keep working.

`update_admin_payment_intent` keeps its current `None`-on-failure contract. The column-absent retry lives inside a nested try and only triggers for the `status_message_id` key; any other error falls through to the outer handler and still returns `None`.
Older deployments that have not re-applied `sql/admin_payment_intents.sql` are transient — the runtime tolerance is a bridge, not a permanent feature flag. No rollback path beyond reverting the code.
No other producers currently rely on the generic `_notify_payment_result` card being suppressed. Only `admin_chat` is silenced.

Exception matching for the column-absent retry uses a simple substring check on the exception text (`'status_message_id' in str(exc)`); Supabase/PostgREST error messages include the column name. Introducing Supabase-specific error classes is out of scope.
